Politics.headline: Trump threatens Iran's oil infrastructure after US bombs strategic island
Politics.detail: President Donald Trump said U.S. forces “obliterated” military targets on Iran's Kharg Island, home to the main terminal that handles the country's oil exports, escalating tensions in the region. Meanwhile, an American official said 2,500 more Marines and an amphibious assault ship are being sent to the Middle East nearly two weeks into the U.S.-Israeli war with the Islamic Republic.

Politics.headline: US embassy in Iraq hit as Mideast War enters third week
Politics.detail: Explosions rocked cities across the Middle East as the US embassy in Baghdad was hit by a drone strike and Tehran warned Emiratis to stay away from ports as it targets the world's energy supply lines. In a statement, the Iraqi military condemned the strike as “a blatant violation of all humanitarian values and a disregard for international conventions.

Politics.headline: GOP, fearing tough fight for Texas Senate seat, paints Democrat as radical
Politics.detail: Republicans are attempting to portray James Talarico, a Democrat running for the U.S. Senate in Texas, as radical, a strategy Talarico dismisses as a distraction from pressing issues. Talarico aims to turn Texas blue in his Senate bid.

Politics.headline: Widespread concern over proposed government involvement in pension investment
Politics.detail: A survey by the ABI reveals that 72% of UK adults have little to no confidence in the government making the right decisions about how their pension is invested if the government is given the power to mandate pension investment. 46% of adults aged over 45 believe that required investment would negatively impact their retirement funds.

Politics.headline: Israel says Iran war entering 'decisive phase'
Politics.detail: As the conflict continues, Israel has announced that the war with Iran is entering a crucial stage. Cities across the Middle East have experienced explosions, and there are increasing concerns about the stability of the region and the potential impact on global energy supplies.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stocks lose ground as war with Iran keeps pressure on oil prices
Finance & Economy.detail: Wall Street's losses deepened as the ongoing fallout from the war in Iran keeps pushing oil prices higher, ratcheting up inflationary pressure on the global economy.

Finance & Economy.headline: Global food supplies could be badly hit if Iran war drags on, says fertiliser boss
Finance & Economy.detail: The ongoing war has caused fertiliser prices to skyrocket just before the spring planting season, which could lead to global food shortages.

Finance & Economy.headline: Oil stays above $100 a barrel amid Iran's stranglehold on Strait of Hormuz
Finance & Economy.detail: Energy markets remain tense as the prospect of a prolonged war in the Middle East grows, keeping oil prices elevated.

Finance & Economy.headline: Fitch raises India FY26 GDP growth forecast to 7.5% on strong domestic demand
Finance & Economy.detail: Fitch Ratings has increased India's GDP growth forecast for fiscal year 2026 to 7.5%, citing strong domestic demand as a key factor.

Finance & Economy.headline: Policymakers around the world are readying measures to absorb surging energy and commodities prices triggered by the Middle East war
Finance & Economy.detail: The Middle East war now threatens the global economy with its biggest shock since the pandemic. Pressure is building in the US for a de-escalation given the mounting death toll and surge in oil prices.

Technology.headline: Apple iPhone Fold Reports Tease a Wider, iPad-Style Interface
Technology.detail: Rumors suggest the upcoming Apple iPhone Fold may feature a wider interface, similar to that of an iPad, offering a unique user experience.

Technology.headline: AMD Pushes a New Category of PCs: The Agent Computer
Technology.detail: AMD is introducing a new category of PCs called 'Agent Computers,' focusing on enhanced AI capabilities and performance.

Technology.headline: Google Maps Just Got a Major AI Upgrade. Here's How 'Ask Maps' Will Work.
Technology.detail: Google Maps has received a significant AI upgrade, introducing a new 'Ask Maps' feature that allows users to interact with the app like a chatbot for more intuitive navigation.

Technology.headline: Flying car dreams gain altitude as Xpeng affiliate Aridge raises US$200 million
Technology.detail: Aridge, an affiliate of Xpeng, has raised US$200 million, signaling growing momentum for flying car technology.

Technology.headline: In a first for China, Neuracle's brain-computer interface wins approval.
Technology.detail: Neuracle, a Chinese company, has received approval for its brain-computer interface, marking a significant step for the country's BCI development.

Science.headline: Textbooks Were Wrong: Scientists Reveal the Surprising Way Human Hair Really Grows
Science.detail: New research indicates that hair growth may occur differently than previously thought, with moving cells inside the follicle pulling the hair instead of it being pushed out from the root.

Science.headline: Scientists May Have Finally Solved the Mystery of Strange Plume-Like Structures Hidden Deep Inside the Greenland Ice Sheet
Science.detail: New research suggests that plume-like structures within the Greenland ice sheet form through thermal convection, which is a slow, swirling process.

Science.headline: A Lab Mistake at Cambridge Reveals a Powerful New Way to Modify Drug Molecules
Science.detail: Cambridge scientists have discovered a light-powered chemical reaction that enables researchers to modify complex drug molecules during the final stages of development.

Science.headline: Scientists Have Uncovered an Enormous Hidden Archive of Plant DNA That Has Endured for More Than 400 Million Years
Science.detail: By comparing hundreds of plant genomes, researchers have identified over 2.3 million segments of conserved non-coding sequences, revealing a hidden archive of plant DNA that has persisted for over 400 million years.

Science.headline: Autism's Link to Parkinson's Risk May Finally Be Explained
Science.detail: A recent study has shed light on the potential connection between autism and the risk of Parkinson's disease, offering a possible explanation for this association.

Health.headline: Gut Bacteria That Make Serotonin May Hold the Key to IBS
Health.detail: Researchers have identified two gut bacteria that can produce serotonin, a key chemical that regulates bowel movements; in experiments with mice lacking serotonin, the microbes boosted serotonin levels and increased nerve cells in the colon.

Health.headline: Microplastics May Be Quietly Damaging Your Brain and Fueling Alzheimer's and Parkinson's
Health.detail: New research suggests microplastics, now widely found in food, water, and even household dust, could trigger inflammation and damage brain health.

Health.headline: Study Finds Two Types of Colon Polyps Can Raise Bowel Cancer Risk Fivefold
Health.detail: Researchers studying over 8,400 colonoscopies discovered that having both adenomas and serrated polyps in the bowel can raise the risk of serious precancerous changes by up to five times.

Health.headline: EPA Aims to Weaken Limits on Cancer-Linked Gas Used to Sterilize Medical Equipment
Health.detail: Long-term exposure can cause cancer among those who work at or live near sterilization facilities, as the EPA is looking to weaken limits on a gas used to sterilize medical equipment.

Health.headline: Mayo Clinic Q&A: Why is fiber good for your overall health?
Health.detail: Megan Lakatos from Mayo Clinic answers the question of why fiber is good for your overall health.

Sports.headline: China beats Chinese Taipei to advance into AFC Women's World Cup semifinals
Sports.detail: China defeated Chinese Taipei 2-0 after extra time in the quarterfinals of the AFC Women's Asian Cup in Perth, Australia on March 14, securing a spot in the semifinals and a ticket to the 2027 Women's World Cup in Brazil. Shao Ziqin scored in the 94th minute, followed by an own goal from Chen Ying-hui.

Sports.headline: Russell beats Ferrari to win Shanghai F1 Sprint
Sports.detail: George Russell of Mercedes won the first Sprint race of the 2026 Formula 1 season at the Shanghai International Circuit on March 14, holding off a challenge from Ferrari. Charles Leclerc finished second, just 0.6 seconds behind Russell, and Lewis Hamilton, also of Ferrari, finished third.

Sports.headline: Barkdull wins indoor national title in pole vault
Sports.detail: Kansas junior Ashton Barkdull won the national championship in pole vault at the indoor championships in Fayetteville, Arkansas, on Friday, March 13, 2026. Barkdull cleared a personal-best 5.80 meters to secure the title.

Sports.headline: Refocused US rebounds from Italy shocker, beats Canada to reach WBC semifinals
Sports.detail: The United States defeated Canada in the World Baseball Classic Quarterfinals. They will advance to the WBC semifinals.

Sports.headline: Parkersburg vs. Parkersburg South in the state championship
Sports.detail: Parkersburg and Parkersburg South will face off in the Class AAAA state championship at 7:30 p.m. today inside the Charleston Coliseum & Convention Center. This is a crosstown showdown between two great teams from one great city, meeting in the ultimate rivalry showdown with a state championship on the line.

Entertainment.headline: Golmaal 5 shoot begins: Rohit Shetty reunites with Ajay Devgn, Akshay Kumar and team; Ranveer Singh reacts
Entertainment.detail: Rohit Shetty has officially announced his next film Golmaal 5 and began shooting for the fifth installment with the OG cast including Ajay Devgn, Arshad Warsi, Kunal Kemmu, Shreyas Talpade and Tusshar Kapoor, with the surprise addition of Akshay Kumar.

Entertainment.headline: Global Village cancels Eid Al Fitr fireworks, remains closed
Entertainment.detail: Dubai's Global Village has announced that their fireworks displays during the upcoming Eid Al Fitr holiday period have been cancelled and will remain closed.

Entertainment.headline: Razzies crown 'War of the Worlds' 2025's worst film
Entertainment.detail: The 2025 American sci-fi film "War of the Worlds" dominated the 46th annual Razzie Awards, taking home worst picture, worst screenplay, worst remake, worst actor and worst director.

Entertainment.headline: Akshay Kumar joins Golmaal 5, Sharman Joshi returns to comedy franchise
Entertainment.detail: Akshay Kumar is officially joining Golmaal 5, and Sharman Joshi is also returning to the comedy franchise.

Entertainment.headline: LPG shortage may stall film productions if crisis continues, say industry leaders
Entertainment.detail: An ongoing LPG shortage is beginning to affect film sets, with productions that depend heavily on gas for daily operations navigating a growing logistical challenge, and industry representatives say a prolonged shortage could eventually disrupt production schedules.

Education.headline: Justice Department charges man accused of selling gun to Old Dominion University shooter
Education.detail: A man has been charged with selling a stolen gun to Mohamed Bailor Jalloh, a former member of the Army National Guard who used it to kill one person and wound two others at Old Dominion University.

Education.headline: Teaching Data From 50 States: Teachers See Student Behavior as a Significant Problem
Education.detail: Teachers across 50 states are reporting that student behavior is a significant problem in schools.

Education.headline: Alberta teachers fail to get court injunction on back-to-work law that ended strike
Education.detail: Alberta teachers were unable to secure a court injunction against the back-to-work law that brought their strike to an end.

Education.headline: Black Students Are the Fastest Growing Demographic for Common App
Education.detail: Black students represent the fastest-growing demographic for the Common Application, signaling potential shifts in college application trends.

Education.headline: Short tempers and legal threats: UK teachers report rise in problem parents
Education.detail: Teachers in the UK are reporting an increase in instances of short tempers and legal threats from parents.

Lifestyle.headline: Pristine waters teeming with marine life: a deep dive into the Greek island of Alonissos.
Lifestyle.detail: Explore the clear waters and abundant marine life surrounding the Greek island of Alonissos, a haven for nature enthusiasts.

Lifestyle.headline: Blind date: 'He looked at me, 5ft, and said he was looking for someone around 5ft 6in'
Lifestyle.detail: A participant shares their experience on a blind date, where their height became an unexpected point of discussion.

Lifestyle.headline: This is how we do it: 'His cancer diagnosis hit the reset button – we've built up quite the collection of toys'
Lifestyle.detail: A couple shares how a cancer diagnosis changed their perspective and led them to embrace life and accumulate a collection of enjoyable items.

Lifestyle.headline: From 'bad decisions' to redemption: Brantley Gilbert launches late Hulk Hogan-inspired non-alcoholic beer.
Lifestyle.detail: Brantley Gilbert has launched a non-alcoholic beer inspired by Hulk Hogan.

Lifestyle.headline: McDonald's CEO roasted after his tiny first bite of new Big Arch burger goes viral.
Lifestyle.detail: The CEO of McDonald's is facing ridicule after a video of his small bite of the new Big Arch burger went viral.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion: An ancient, sophisticated palate
Opinion/Editorial.detail: New research suggests that our prehistoric ancestors had more varied and cosmopolitan tastes than previously thought, with evidence of selective cooking and 'recipes' using plants, fish, and berries dating back 5,000 to 8,000 years in northern Europe. It appears ancient humans didn't just eat roasted meat but enjoyed sophisticated dishes.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion: Symptoms of political illness
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The author argues that the current political climate exhibits symptoms of impending autocracy, such as substituting the president's name for the country's and displaying self-aggrandizing banners. These actions are presented as warning signs that the nation's democratic foundation is under threat.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Amber alert for native wildlife
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The editorial warns that deforestation in Sumatra is endangering native wildlife like elephants and tigers, and Papua is at risk of following a similar path. It criticizes the prioritization of short-term profits over rainforest preservation and the perception of wildlife as pests.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Yes-sentials: On the people around us
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This opinion piece likely reflects on the importance of human connections and relationships in our lives. It is published in The Daily Northwestern.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Spies in plain sight
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses the Alice Guo case, highlighting it as a template for illegally granting Filipino citizenship to Chinese nationals involved in criminal activities such as human trafficking and cryptocurrency scams. The former mayor was also identified as a fellow operative for China by a Chinese businessman.

World.headline: All 6 crew members on a US refueling plane that crashed in Iraq are dead, US military says
World.detail: The U.S. military has confirmed that all six crew members were killed when an American KC-135 refueling plane taking part in the Iran war crashed in western Iraq. The cause of the crash is currently unknown.

World.headline: Trump says US forces destroyed military targets on Iranian island handling oil exports
World.detail: President Trump stated that the U.S. military conducted bombing raids on Kharg Island, a vital Iranian oil hub. Kharg Island handles about 90 percent of Iran's oil exports.

World.headline: Explosion damages Jewish school in Amsterdam
World.detail: An explosion damaged a Jewish school in Amsterdam, but there were no injuries. The cause of the explosion is under investigation.

World.headline: Iraqi Prime Minister and French President discuss regional developments after French soldier killed
World.detail: Iraqi Prime Minister Mohammed Shia' al-Sudani and French President Emmanuel Macron discussed regional developments following an attack that resulted in the death of a French soldier in northern Iraq. The phone call occurred on Friday.

World.headline: US Embassy in Baghdad, Iraq attacked with missile that hits helipad.
World.detail: The US Embassy in Baghdad, Iraq was attacked with a missile that hit the helipad, causing damage to the mission. Smoke was seen rising from the building after the attack.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zim Treasury to pay local suppliers in ZiG under new price guidelines
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwean treasury will be paying local suppliers in ZiG, the new currency, under new price guidelines.

Zimbabwe.headline: High Court stays Magaya rape trial
Zimbabwe.detail: The High Court has put a stay on Prophet Magaya's rape trial.

Zimbabwe.headline: Violence and Intimidation Against Opponents of Presidential Term Extension
Zimbabwe.detail: Authorities in Zimbabwe have intensified their crackdown against critics of an effort by the ruling Zimbabwe African National to extend the presidential term.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe looks to Ethiopia as model for modernized judiciary
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwean Chief Justice Luke Malaba has lauded Ethiopia's judiciary as a trailblazer.

Zimbabwe.headline: Daily News, Financial Gazette publishers lose Labour Court appeal over minimum wages
Zimbabwe.detail: The publishers of the Daily News and Financial Gazette lost a Labour Court appeal regarding minimum wages.
